# Copilot Instructions for FabricaHilos

## Project Overview

FabricaHilos ("Thread Factory" in Spanish) is a project in its early stages. This file provides guidance for GitHub Copilot coding agent to work efficiently in this repository.

## Repository Structure

- `README.md` — Project description and documentation.
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — This file; Copilot-specific instructions.

## Development Guidelines

- Keep code well-documented, especially public APIs and non-obvious logic.
- Write clear commit messages that describe *what* and *why*.
- Prefer small, focused pull requests over large, sweeping changes.

## Coding Conventions

- Follow the conventions already established in any existing source files.
- Use descriptive variable and function names.
- Add comments where the intent is not immediately obvious.

## Testing

- Add tests for any new functionality when a test framework is present.
- Ensure all existing tests pass before opening a pull request.

## Build & Validation

- If build or CI scripts are added, document the commands needed to build, lint, and test in this file or in the README.
